locations.name: Saltmere Docks
locations.government: Nominally overseen by the Harbor Warden's Office, but the dockworkers' guild runs the day-to-day business of the wharves.
locations.populationDiversity: Mostly human and dwarf laborers, with a growing halfling merchant community and the occasional half-orc deckhand passing through.
locations.languages: Common and Dwarvish are everywhere; old sailors still trade a few words of Aquan out of habit.
locations.resources: Salted fish, whale oil, rope and tar, and a steady supply of dockside labor for hire.
locations.funFact: The harbor bell hasn't been recast in two hundred years — sailors swear that replacing it would anger the tide.
locations.description: A sprawl of weathered piers, salt-bleached warehouses, and narrow streets that smell of brine and tar. Saltmere has grown up around its harbor rather than the other way around, and everything in it — the buildings, the people, the rules — bends to the rhythm of the tides and the fishing fleets.
locations.sensoryDetail: Gulls shriek over the wharves. Rigging creaks against the wind, and somewhere a bell buoy tolls low and slow. The air tastes of salt, fish guts, and tar smoke from the cooper's yards.
locations.hiddenDetail: Beneath the old chapel at the top of Lantern Hill is a sealed crypt that predates the town itself — sealed not to keep grave robbers out, but to keep something in.

districts.name: The Lower Wharves
districts.readAloud: The boards underfoot are slick with seawater and fish scale, and every other building leans a little too far toward the harbor, propped up with timber struts and stubbornness.

pointsOfInterest.name: The Ashen Hearth
pointsOfInterest.description: A dockside tavern run by a stocky dwarf named Barrin Soot, who's heard every rumor that's passed through Saltmere in the last thirty years — for the right favor, he might share one.

pointsOfInterest.name: Guildmaster's Scales
pointsOfInterest.description: The dockworkers' guild hall, where the day's catch is weighed, wages are argued over, and grudges are occasionally settled with fists out back.

districts.name: Lantern Hill
districts.readAloud: The streets climb sharply away from the harbor here, lined with narrow houses and the old chapel whose spire has guided ships home for three hundred years.

pointsOfInterest.name: Chapel of the Drowned Light
pointsOfInterest.description: A small, weather-beaten chapel tended by a single aging cleric. Its bell hasn't rung in living memory, and the locals prefer it that way.

locations.onTheHorizon: The grave stirs — for the past week, dockworkers have found fresh grave dirt scattered across the chapel steps each morning, though no one has been buried on Lantern Hill in decades.
